facilities.title: My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd is a Petaling Jaya eldercare operator founded in 2008 that runs a cluster of licensed nursing homes across PJ. Three branches are documented in this directory: My Manor in PJS 7, PJ Old Town along Jalan Othman, and the Jalan Assunta branch. All three sit within a compact radius of each other in the Petaling Jaya healthcare corridor, making My Aged Care one of the more geographically concentrated multi-branch nursing-home operators in the Klang Valley. The group is built around caring for elderly residents with active clinical needs rather than assisted-living support.

My Aged Care's care scope covers residents with active nursing needs: bedridden patients, post-stroke recovery, palliative support, and dementia management. Night-shift nursing rounds are conducted hourly across all branches — a published operational standard that is materially different from homes running four-hourly checks. Physiotherapy is available through MAC Physio Corner, the group's in-house unit. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, varying by branch and care level. My Aged Care holds JKM licensing for its nursing home branches.

Families choosing between the three branches should weigh specific priorities. My Manor offers the most space in a low-density PJS 7 setting built around fewer residents per floor. PJ Old Town provides a familiar town-centre neighbourhood with good vehicle access and pharmacy proximity. The Jalan Assunta branch sits directly adjacent to Assunta Hospital, making it the strongest choice when ongoing hospital coordination — specialist outpatient visits, diagnostics, or emergency step-down transfers — is a regular requirement. A single enquiry to the group's contact line will give an overview of availability and pricing across all three simultaneously. Ask about each branch's nurse-to-resident ratio on nights, the in-house clinical procedures each manages, and how they handle a care escalation situation.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ My Manor is the flagship branch of My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd, a licensed nursing-home operator established in 2008 in Petaling Jaya. The home occupies a roughly 10,000-square-foot property in PJS 7, positioned behind the MBPJ City Council building — a spacious, low-density setting built around the concept of fewer residents per floor than a conventional multi-storey nursing home. The group has built its practice around caring for residents with active nursing needs, and My Manor reflects that clinical orientation.

My Aged Care's care scope covers residents with active nursing needs: bedridden patients, post-stroke recovery, palliative support, and dementia management. Night-shift nursing rounds are conducted hourly across all branches — a published operational standard that is materially different from homes running four-hourly checks. Physiotherapy is available through MAC Physio Corner, the group's in-house unit. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, varying by branch and care level. My Aged Care holds JKM licensing for its nursing home branches.

With 142 Google reviews at 4.5 stars, My Manor is one of the more thoroughly documented nursing homes in the western PJ corridor. The reviewer volume gives families a realistic picture of day-to-day experience rather than a single-source impression. Reading the most recent 20–30 reviews before your visit will surface patterns in staffing responsiveness and communication with families. On the visit itself, ask about the nurse-to-resident ratio at night, which clinical procedures are managed in-house (wound care, PEG feeds, catheter changes, oxygen therapy), how a deteriorating resident's care escalation is managed, and what the monthly fee breakdown looks like with all extras listed.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ PJ Old Town is one of three nursing-home branches operated by My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd across Petaling Jaya. The home sits along Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus in PJ Old Town — one of PJ's most established neighbourhoods — within easy reach of Assunta Hospital and the broader PJ medical cluster. My Aged Care was founded in 2008 around caring for clinically complex elderly residents: bedridden care, post-stroke management, palliative support, and dementia are all within the group's scope.

My Aged Care's care scope covers residents with active nursing needs: bedridden patients, post-stroke recovery, palliative support, and dementia management. Night-shift nursing rounds are conducted hourly across all branches — a published operational standard that is materially different from homes running four-hourly checks. Physiotherapy is available through MAC Physio Corner, the group's in-house unit. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, varying by branch and care level. My Aged Care holds JKM licensing for its nursing home branches.

The PJ Old Town branch has accumulated 92 Google reviews at 4.8 stars — among the stronger nursing-home ratings in this corridor. A high score sustained across a significant reviewer base in a high-dependency setting carries more weight than a similar rating at a lower-acuity assisted-living facility. PJ Old Town suits families who want a mature-neighbourhood nursing environment with strong hospital proximity. On your visit, confirm the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shifts, ask which specific clinical procedures are managed in-house, and request the JKM licence number. If your family member has a language preference — Mandarin, Cantonese, or Tamil — confirm whether the current staff mix can accommodate it.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ Jalan Assunta is positioned at 9 Jalan Assunta 4/52 in Petaling Jaya, directly adjacent to Assunta Hospital — one of the most clinically useful locations a nursing home can occupy in the Klang Valley. The proximity is not incidental: this branch of My Aged Care Sdn. Bhd serves residents whose care requires ongoing hospital-side coordination, whether for specialist outpatient appointments, diagnostic procedures, or emergency step-down following hospitalisation. The group has operated in PJ since 2008 with a focus on residents who need active nursing management.

My Aged Care's care scope covers residents with active nursing needs: bedridden patients, post-stroke recovery, palliative support, and dementia management. Night-shift nursing rounds are conducted hourly across all branches — a published operational standard that is materially different from homes running four-hourly checks. Physiotherapy is available through MAC Physio Corner, the group's in-house unit. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range, varying by branch and care level. My Aged Care holds JKM licensing for its nursing home branches.

The Assunta Hospital adjacency makes this the strongest My Aged Care branch to consider when the resident's care plan involves regular specialist input — post-oncology, complex cardiac, chronic renal, or major post-surgical cases where outpatient coordination is ongoing. The walking distance between the nursing home and Assunta's ward block removes a meaningful logistical burden for families. With 85 Google reviews at 4.4 stars, it is a well-documented option in the Assunta corridor. Ask on your visit about the emergency handover protocol, how often a GP visits the branch, which clinical procedures are managed in-house versus transferred out, and what the monthly fee breakdown includes across all chargeable items.

facilities.title: Amazing Grace Senior Home
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Senior Home is a residential eldercare facility in SS19, Subang Jaya, operated under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand. The same operator runs a companion property in Section 14, Petaling Jaya — the two homes together form one of the most consistently reviewed eldercare operations in the Petaling Jaya–Subang Jaya corridor. The SS19 address sits in a well-established residential neighbourhood with good access to Sunway Medical Centre, Subang Jaya Medical Centre, and the broader Subang–PJ hospital network.

With 156 Google reviews at a sustained 5.0-star rating, Amazing Grace Senior Home carries one of the largest and most consistent social proof records for any nursing or retirement home in this catchment area. A review base of this size, built across multiple years, points to genuine family confidence in day-to-day care quality rather than a single good period. Specific services, clinical scope, bed count, staffing ratios, and monthly fees are not published publicly. Based on third-party category listings, the home is positioned as an assisted-living and retirement residence rather than a high-dependency clinical nursing facility.

This home suits families whose priority is a warm, community-focused residential environment in Subang Jaya — for elderly residents who need structured daily care, social engagement, and a reliable family-visiting culture rather than round-the-clock acute clinical monitoring. If the resident has medical needs that may intensify over time — advanced dementia, bedridden care, palliative stage — confirm on the visit what the home can and cannot manage in-house. Ask about daily activity programming, night-shift staffing levels, the JKM licence number, how a medical escalation is handled, and the full fee structure by room type and care level.

facilities.title: Amazing Grace Retirement Home
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Retirement Home sits at 11 Jalan 14/55 in Section 14, Petaling Jaya — a mature, tree-lined residential neighbourhood within a short drive of Damansara Specialist Hospital and the central PJ medical corridor. It is one of two properties operated under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, alongside a sister home in SS19, Subang Jaya. The Section 14 setting has the character of a house in an established neighbourhood rather than an institutional facility, which often suits families whose relative is making a first move into residential care.

With 140 Google reviews at 4.9 stars, Amazing Grace Retirement Home carries one of the strongest social proof records for any nursing or retirement home in the Petaling Jaya market. A reviewer base of this scale, consistently rated across multiple years, suggests the home has maintained stable care quality through staff changes and different occupancy periods. Specific services, clinical scope, bed count, and monthly fees are not published online. Families considering placement should discuss clinical capabilities directly with the home, particularly for residents whose medical needs may increase over time.

Amazing Grace Section 14 suits families looking for a warm, residential eldercare environment in central PJ — particularly for seniors transitioning from independent living who benefit from structured daily support and social engagement. The Section 14 location is convenient for families across the PJ corridor with good road links toward Bangsar, Subang, and Damansara. On your visit, ask about daily programming and activities, the nurse-to-caregiver ratio at night, what nursing procedures are available in-house, the JKM licence number, and how they handle a resident whose care needs escalate significantly after admission.

facilities.title: FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru
facilities.editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre occupies a campus on Jalan Resam in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram, on the eastern outskirts of Johor Bahru. The operator positions FCC as Malaysia's first integrated senior living and wellness village — a description that reflects the campus design: an edible garden where residents plant and harvest, free-roaming animals, healing walkways, a library, craft rooms, and open communal spaces alongside nursing care. In 2020, FCC became the first Malaysian retirement village to deploy SmartPeep, a Singapore-developed AI vision-monitoring system that detects falls and unusual movement patterns overnight.

The campus operates three service tiers: stay-in residential care, day care with meals and basic medical support, and rehabilitation and physiotherapy. Within the residential wing, FCC distinguishes between The CHS Home for general residential residents and a separate MemoryCare Home providing specialist dementia care in a dedicated environment. This three-tier structure means families can transition a relative from day care into residential or rehabilitation within the same operator as needs change. Pricing, capacity, and JKM licence details are not published on the operator website — request these directly when making contact.

FCC suits families drawn to a nature-based, lower-stimulation living environment alongside nursing support — particularly early-to-mid-stage dementia, post-rehabilitation recovery, and seniors who would struggle in a conventional ward-style facility. The 4.9-star rating from 137 Google reviews reflects consistent praise built over several years, with recurring mentions of the gardens, animals, and staff approach. The Ulu Tiram location is practical for families in the eastern JB corridor but involves a longer drive from the city centre or Iskandar Puteri. On your visit, confirm the clinical capability for high-dependency residents — PEG, oxygen dependency, complex wound care — the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, and how SmartPeep alerts are triaged after hours.

facilities.title: FCC Family Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
This listing corresponds to the day care and community services function of the FCC Family Care Centre campus in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram, Johor Bahru. Both this entry and the FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru residential listing refer to the same physical campus — the two records reflect separate service registrations operating at the same site. Families researching day care, short-stay, or residential options at FCC will find the same grounds, operator, and staff team described under both profiles.

FCC's day care programme draws on the campus's edible gardens, farm animals, library, craft facilities, and structured activity schedule to offer a stimulating daytime programme for elderly residents who remain at home. The campus philosophy — environmental enrichment and community activity alongside nursing oversight — is particularly suited to early-stage dementia and socially isolated seniors who benefit from structured engagement during the day. The campus deployed SmartPeep AI monitoring in 2020 for overnight safety in the residential wing; day care participants benefit from the same safety infrastructure during their hours on site. Pricing for day care should be confirmed with FCC directly.

FCC's day care option suits families whose parent or relative can still live at home but benefits from a structured, stimulating daytime programme — and where informal family care alone is no longer sufficient during working hours. The Ulu Tiram location is most convenient for families in the eastern JB corridor. For families considering a transition from day care into full residential stay, the FCC campus offers that path within the same operator and same environment. On your initial call, confirm current day care availability, programme hours, transport arrangements, and what medical services — wound dressings, blood pressure monitoring, medication administration — are included in the day rate.

facilities.title: Genesis Life Care Centre PJ
facilities.editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre PJ is the flagship branch of the Genesis Life Care group, a multi-branch nursing home operator with locations across Selangor and Johor Bahru. The PJ centre occupies a purpose-built space at Lot 1, Petaling Utama Avenue, Jalan PJS 1/50 in Taman Petaling Utama, Petaling Jaya — established in 2020, making it the newest of the group's Klang Valley branches and the one built to the group's current design standards. With 50-plus beds and a dedicated memory care wing, it serves as the group's flagship in the PJ area.

Genesis Life Care holds JKM licensing and AgeCope certification chain-wide. The published care scope covers long-term nursing, dementia and memory care with a psychologist-led programme, post-stroke rehabilitation, palliative and end-of-life support, post-operative recovery, and respite admissions. Weekly GP visits and physiotherapy are standard across the chain. The PJ branch includes a dedicated memory care wing and an on-site rehabilitation suite. Pricing for the PJ branch is not published on the operator website — request a written fee schedule that separates the base monthly rate from add-on charges for therapy, specialist medications, and consumable items.

Genesis PJ suits families in the Petaling Jaya or Subang Jaya area looking for a purpose-built, professionally managed nursing home with a group structure behind it. The Taman Petaling Utama address has federal-highway access and parking. With 22 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, the PJ branch is still building its reviewer base relative to the more established Klang flagship — the Klang branch's longer review trail gives a better picture of the group's operational style over time. On your visit, ask to see the memory care wing specifically if dementia is the primary reason for placement, confirm what is and is not included in the monthly fee, and request the JKM licence number and AgeCope certification documentation.

facilities.title: Genesis Life Care Centre Kajang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre Kajang is located at 41 Jalan Ria 1 in Kawasan Perindustrian Ria — an industrial estate off the Cheras–Kajang Highway, opened in 2021 as one of the Genesis Life Care group's Klang Valley branches. The industrial setting trades aesthetic surroundings for practical accessibility: wide approach roads, ample parking, and direct highway access from Cheras, Bangi, Kajang town, and Semenyih make this branch straightforward for families spread across the southeast Klang Valley.

Genesis Life Care holds JKM licensing and AgeCope certification chain-wide. The published care scope covers long-term nursing, dementia and memory care with a psychologist-led programme, post-stroke rehabilitation, palliative and end-of-life support, post-operative recovery, and respite admissions. Weekly GP visits and physiotherapy are standard across the chain. Being in an industrial park rather than a prime residential address keeps operational costs lower, which may translate to more competitive pricing relative to the PJ-corridor branches — confirm the actual rate during your visit, as pricing is not posted publicly. Request a full written fee schedule that separates the base monthly charge from extras including physiotherapy sessions, specialist medications, and consumables.

This branch serves the Kajang, Cheras, Bangi, and Semenyih corridor effectively. Families who find the PJ or KL city-centre branches logistically difficult for regular visiting will find Kajang's highway location more practical. With 37 Google reviews, the Kajang branch has a smaller reviewer base than the founding Klang location, which opened three years earlier. Ask on your visit about night-shift nursing ratios, the dementia care programme in practice, and which clinical procedures are handled in-house. Hospital Kajang is the nearest government emergency referral centre.

facilities.title: Genesis Life Care Centre Klang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care Centre Klang is the founding branch of the Genesis Life Care group, established in 2018 at No. 3, Jalan Istana, Amverton Business Centre, Klang. As the original branch, Klang has the longest operational track record in the chain — over six years in the market — and sits close to Tengku Ampuan Rahimah (TAR) Hospital, the main government referral hospital for Klang. The TAR Hospital proximity is a practical advantage for residents who need regular specialist follow-up or rapid emergency access.

Genesis Life Care holds JKM licensing and AgeCope certification chain-wide. The published care scope covers long-term nursing, dementia and memory care with a psychologist-led programme, post-stroke rehabilitation, palliative and end-of-life support, post-operative recovery, and respite admissions. Weekly GP visits and physiotherapy are standard across the chain. With 71 Google reviews, Klang is the most thoroughly reviewed branch in the chain, reflecting six-plus years of family placements — the review volume gives prospective families a richer picture of the group's operational style than the newer branches. Pricing for the Klang branch is not posted on the operator website — request a written fee breakdown, including the base monthly rate and all add-on charges, during your site visit.

Genesis Klang suits families in the Klang, Meru, Bukit Raja, or western Shah Alam corridor who need a fully licensed, operationally mature nursing home with hospital proximity. As the founding branch, it is likely to have the most experienced staff tenure within the group. Ask about the night-shift nurse-to-resident ratio, the dementia unit configuration, and which clinical procedures are handled in-house versus referred out. If the resident is transferring from TAR Hospital, ask about the discharge-to-admission coordination process and how quickly a bed can be confirmed.

facilities.title: Genesis Life Care JB
facilities.editorial_summary:
Genesis Life Care JB is the Johor Bahru branch of the Genesis Life Care chain, opened in April 2025 at Level 6, Holiday Plaza Tower, Jalan Dato Sulaiman in Taman Century. The location is central JB — close to City Square and the Causeway corridor, with commercial services on lower floors of the same building. The campus occupies a full floor of the tower and advertises 120-plus beds, making it one of the larger new nursing-home openings in the JB market in 2025.

Genesis Life Care holds JKM licensing and AgeCope certification chain-wide. The published care scope covers long-term nursing, dementia and memory care with a psychologist-led programme, post-stroke rehabilitation, palliative and end-of-life support, post-operative recovery, and respite admissions. Weekly GP visits and physiotherapy are standard across the chain. The operator's JB branch page advertises residential packages from RM 2,500 per month with halal dining available. As a newly opened branch, staffing levels and clinical capability are still in an early operational phase relative to the mature Klang and Kajang locations. Families should verify the current care scope and nurse-to-resident ratios in person rather than relying on chain-level published services.

Because this branch opened in April 2025, an in-person walkthrough is essential before any commitment. A full-floor commercial tower running 120 beds is an unusual configuration for JB, and it is worth counting beds and rooms yourself during the visit. Ask the duty charge nurse what the current nurse-to-resident ratio is on day and night shifts, confirm the JKM licence number specifically assigned to this Holiday Plaza address, and pin down what the RM 2,500 starting rate excludes — consumables, diapers, physiotherapy sessions, and specialist medications are commonly billed separately. Hospital Sultan Ismail, approximately 3 km away, is the nearest government emergency referral centre.
